park_wind_profile.wind_note: Chavez Ravine bowl geometry suppresses surface winds significantly. Marine layer from the Pacific creates dense, cool air that counteracts OUT winds. Wind rarely changes scoring dynamics here.

park_wind_profile.wind_note: Oracle_Dead_Pocket: The famous 'dead pocket' in the right-center gap is created by wind off San Francisco Bay (generally from the W/NW) being deflected by AT&T Park's architecture. Due-East CF (90Â°) faces the bay, but the cold dense marine air and the swirling pocket reduce the effective OUT wind impact by ~40% vs a naive vector calculation. Cross-bay wind increases air density, partially SUPPRESSING power despite the apparent OUT direction. Flag as Oracle_Dead_Pocket when wind_bearing 270-330 â€” reduced, not enhanced.
